reforms.description: The 1975 Budget announced an annual rate 20 percent increase in the public capital programme, focusing on building and construction, particularly local authority financed housing, along with significant increases for electricity supply and telephone development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in infrastructure such as housing and utilities are expected to stimulate economic activity and improve living standards, thereby supporting growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The annual rate 20 percent increase in the public capital programme was announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in public infrastructure, which is crucial for economic development and can have lasting impacts on various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: An annual rate 20 percent increase in the public capital programme was announced, the main component increase being in building and construction, in particular local authority financed housing.
